results.title: REHVA Journal Measurement and Quantification of Thermal Environments; ISO 7726 -Ergonomics of the Thermal Environment - Instruments and methods for measuring and monitoring physical quantities
results.description: The time taken for a measurement ... within a suitable timeframe. ISO 7726 provides standardised conditions and procedure for the determination of time constants for instruments....
results.content:
The time taken for a measurement ... within a suitable timeframe. ISO 7726 provides standardised conditions and procedure for the determination of time constants for instruments....
ISO 7726 -Ergonomics of the thermal environment - Instruments and methods for measuring and monitoring physical quantities specifies the minimum characteristics of instruments for measuring physical quantities characterizing an environment as well as the methods for measuring the physical quantities of this environment.
When selecting measurement instruments for the evaluation of the thermal environment, particularly those where people will be working or living it is important that they have an appropriate level of accuracy. Accuracy defines how close the reported measured values are to the true value. ISO 7726 provides required accuracy tolerances and desirable tolerances.
The time taken for a measurement instrument to reach a stable output within the environment also important. Any instrument needs to respond within a suitable timeframe. ISO 7726 provides standardised conditions and procedure for the determination of time constants for instruments.
ISO 7726 also considers the practicalities of measuring the physical quantities in real world situations. An environment may be considered to be homogeneous if, air temperature, mean radiant temperature, mean air velocity and partial vapour pressure are practically uniform around the person.

results.title: (PDF) Measurement and Quantification of Thermal Environments; ISO 7726 -Ergonomics of the Thermal Environment - Instruments and methods for measuring and monitoring physical quantities
results.description: ISO 7726 standard describes both the measurement techniques and the related metrological performances of instruments required for the assessment of the thermal environment.
results.content:
ISO 7726 standard describes both the measurement techniques and the related metrological performances of instruments required for the assessment of the thermal environment.
The methodology includes a review of international and national regulatory frameworks (ISO 7730, EN 16798-1, DBN V.2.5-67:2013, DSN 3.3.6.042-99), instrumental measurements in accordance with ISO 7726, heat and moisture balance calculations, determination of air exchange rates for removing excess heat and water vapour, as well as design solutions for general and local ventilation, air-curtain systems, and dust aspiration.
... Thermal comfort assessment commonly references American National Standards Institute/ American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers (ANSI/ASHRAE) Standard 55 [34] and International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 7726 [35], which define acceptable environmental parameter combinations and measurement protocols.
[3]. Standards such as ISO 7726 and ASHRAE Standard 55 provide widely accepted frameworks for assessing comfort, primarily through indices such as Predicted Mean Vote (PMV) and Predicted Percentage Dissatisfied (PPD).
